name: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
description: Yuval Noah Harari's sweeping journey through the history of our species, from Stone Age foragers to modern Homo sapiens.

reviews.title: Brilliant and eye-opening
reviews.reviewText: Harari covers 70,000 years of human history with clarity and insight. Each chapter contains ideas that fundamentally shifted how I think about money, religion, and civilisation. One of the most important books I have ever read.

reviews.title: Fascinating, readable history
reviews.reviewText: Harari has a rare gift for making big ideas accessible without losing depth. I read this in four days and recommended it to everyone I know. Absolutely riveting.

reviews.title: Thought-provoking and broad in scope
reviews.reviewText: An ambitious attempt to distil human history and it largely succeeds. Some sections are more convincing than others but the overall effect is a perspective shift on what it means to be human.
